svg.title: Orbit — Presence and Typing Indicator Flow
svg.text: SEQUENCE DIAGRAM — PRESENCE AND TYPING INDICATOR FLOW
WebSocket connection established — JWT validated, STOMP CONNECTED frame sent (see websocket_lifecycle.md Phase 1)
build presence event { userId, online: true, lastSeen }
3. publish → chat.presence { userId, online: true, lastSeen }
4. event stored on chat.presence partition (Upstash Kafka)
Presence events are NOT persisted beyond the users.online flag. They are ephemeral Kafka events consumed and discarded after WebSocket delivery.
FLOW B — TYPING INDICATOR (ephemeral — no DB write, short TTL)
Triggered when user starts typing. React SPA sends a STOMP SEND frame every 2 seconds while typing continues. No MongoDB write at any point.
NO MongoDB write — typing is purely ephemeral
3. publish → chat.presence { conversationId, userId, displayName, typing: true }
When user stops typing, React SPA sends { typing: false } after a 3-second debounce. Same Kafka publish and WebSocket delivery occur.
Recipients auto-clear the indicator after 5 seconds even without a { typing: false } event — guards against missed stop events on disconnect.

g.text: FLOW C — USER GOES OFFLINE (clean disconnect — STOMP DISCONNECT received)
1. STOMP DISCONNECT frame (or browser beforeunload event)
4. publish → chat.presence { userId, online: false, lastSeen }
contact's green dot turns grey instantly
REST logout calls POST /api/v1/auth/logout before DISCONNECT — tokens are blacklisted before the session is torn down. See websocket_lifecycle.md Phase 5.
FLOW D — UNEXPECTED DISCONNECT (network drop / heartbeat timeout)
No STOMP DISCONNECT received. Server detects dead session via heartbeat timeout (50s = 2 × 25s interval).
3. publish → chat.presence { userId, online: false, lastSeen }
Steps 2–5 are identical to Flow C. The only difference is the trigger — heartbeat timeout instead of a clean DISCONNECT frame.
Instance A (user's WebSocket session connects here)
Instance B (contact's session may be here)
1. PresenceService on Instance A handles connect/disconnect
4. ALL instances consume from chat.presence — each checks its local session registry
Instance A: checks registry → delivers to any contact sessions it holds
Instance B: checks registry → delivers to any contact sessions it holds
▸ Presence events publish to chat.presence — separate from chat.messages. Short retention configured on this topic (minutes not days) since events are ephemeral.
▸ Typing indicators are never persisted to MongoDB. They are the lightest events in the system — pure Kafka publish → WebSocket delivery with no DB involvement.
▸ users.online and lastSeen are the only presence fields persisted. Both updated on connect and disconnect by PresenceService via UserRepository.
▸ Multiple open tabs: online is set to false only when ALL sessions for that userId disconnect — tracked by counting active sessions per userId in WebSocketConfig.
▸ Contacts who are offline when a presence event fires simply miss it. On next connect they fetch current status via GET /api/v1/contacts — users.online provides current state.
▸ React SPA subscribes to /topic/presence/{contactId} per contact on connect (websocket_lifecycle.md Phase 2, step 8). Re-established on every reconnection.
